description_long: This object is a vintage television set with a rectangular wooden exterior and a prominent, slightly convex glass screen. The front panel features a metallic frame around the screen, two large circular dials for channel and volume control, and a vertical speaker grille. The top and sides are finished with a wood grain texture, while the back is made of a dark, marbled plastic or Bakelite material with ventilation slots and a label reading 'Televizor.' The overall design is boxy and robust, typical of mid-20th-century televisions, with visible screws and seams indicating a sturdy construction. The television's controls and speaker are all located on the right side of the front panel, and there are no visible modern ports or flat-panel features.
description: A vintage, boxy television with a wood grain exterior, convex glass screen, and front-facing dials and speaker grille.
description_view_0: The left side view shows the wood grain paneling with no visible controls or screen.
description_view_1: The front view displays the convex glass screen, two large dials, and a vertical speaker grille, all framed by a metallic bezel.
description_view_2: The right side view mirrors the left, showing only the wood grain paneling.
description_view_3: The back view reveals a dark marbled plastic panel with ventilation slots and a central label marked 'Televizor.'
attribute_bezel_design: Thick, metallic gray bezel with a matte finish and squared edges, surrounding the screen and controls.
attribute_control_button_layout: Two large circular dials and a vertical speaker grille on the right side of the front panel.
